New Zealand · Southern Alps · 29 June – 9 July 2026

The Bowkers
do winter.

Ten nights, two lakeside basecamps and a whole lot of snow — Queenstown and Wānaka, the family ski trip edition.

ski fields on tap
1
epic family week
The shape of it

Two towns, one big snow week.

We fly into Queenstown for the buzz, the gondola and the easy-access slopes — then drive over the Crown Range to quieter, lake-hugging Wānaka for the back half. Skiing flexes with the forecast; the rest is here to enjoy.

Basecamp One

Queenstown

Mon 29 Jun – Sun 5 Jul. Right in the middle of town, walk everywhere, ski two mountains and ride the rivers to Arrowtown.

Home base · BayCentre By The Lake, 62 Shotover St — two minutes from Fergburger.
Basecamp Two

Wānaka

Sun 5 Jul – Thu 9 Jul. Slower, prettier, big-mountain skiing at Treble Cone and that famous lake-tree on the doorstep.

Home base · Little Holly House, Hedditch St — a short stroll to the lakefront.
Day by day

The draft schedule.

A loose plan, not a timetable — we chase the sunny days onto the snow and save hot pools, the lake and the bike for the cloudy ones.

Queenstown · 6 nights
29
Mon Jun
Touch down in Queenstown
Land at 8pm, settle into the apartment in the heart of town.
Arrive
30
Tue Jun
First tracks
Coronet Peak or The Remarkables — whichever's looking good.
SkiWeather pick
01
Wed Jul
More mountain · Night Ski under lights
Ski by day, then floodlit runs, fire pits and pizza at Coronet (optional).
Ski
02
Thu Jul
Jet boat & hot pools
Shotover Jet through the canyons, then a private soak at Onsen — both at Arthurs Point. (Bluebird? Ski instead.)
Adventure
03
Fri Jul
Last quiet day on the snow
Quietest skiing of the trip — and a second crack at Night Ski.
Ski
04
Sat Jul
Pedal to Arrowtown
Family e-bike along the rivers — a long lunch at Ayrburn by Lake Hayes, then gold-rush Arrowtown.
Adventure
Over the Crown Range · travel day
05
Sun Jul
Queenstown → Wānaka
Pick up the car, climb the Crown Range, lunch at the historic Cardrona Hotel, hello Wānaka.
Road trip
Wānaka · 4 nights
06
Mon Jul
Wānaka snow
Treble Cone for the big terrain and lake views, or Cardrona's wide groomers.
SkiWeather pick
07
Tue Jul
Second day on the snow
Treble Cone or Cardrona again — back-to-back ski days.
Ski
08
Wed Jul
Lake day — or one more run
The famous tree, a walk and hot chocolate. Snow next-level? Bank a bonus ski day.
Lake or ski
09
Thu Jul
Homeward bound
Early drive back to Queenstown and fly home — tired and happy.
Depart
☼ How the flex works: the bold-dated ski days move with the forecast. Blue sky? We're on the mountain. Socked in? That's a hot-pools, lake or town day. Nothing's locked except the flights, the drive and the e-bike.
Basecamp One

Queenstown

Adventure capital of New Zealand, wrapped around Lake Wakatipu. We're staying dead-central and going car-free — coaches to the mountains, everything else on foot.

On the snow

  • Coronet Peak — closest field (~25 min), and the only one with Night Ski.
  • The Remarkables — sunny bowls and chutes, ~45 min up.
  • Pick on the day — one pass covers both mountains.

Off the snow

  • Skyline Gondola & luge — the big view, and racing the luge.
  • Onsen Hot Pools — private tubs over the Shotover canyon.
  • Shotover Jet — 360° canyon spins at 90km/h.
  • Arrowtown e-bike & Ayrburn — the rivers ride and a long lunch, our Saturday.
Where we're staying
BayCentre By The Lake · 62 Shotover Street — in the thick of it, a two-minute walk to Fergburger and the lakefront.
Basecamp Two

Wānaka

Queenstown's calmer, prettier cousin an hour over the hill. Big-mountain skiing, a glassy lake, and that one very famous tree.

On the snow

  • Treble Cone — the South Island's biggest vertical, steep and uncrowded, huge lake views.
  • Cardrona — wide groomers, terrain parks and the new Soho Basin.
  • Either, any day — one pass covers both.

Lake & land

  • That Wānaka Tree — the lone willow in the lake, best at golden hour.
  • Rippon & the lakefront — easy strolls and big views.
  • Diamond Lake walk — a leg-stretch on a rest day.
Where we're staying
Little Holly House · Hedditch Street — a short walk from the lake and town.
At the table

Where we’ll eat.

A table for every night — choose by mood and what the day held. Book the splurges ahead; in peak season they go first.

Queenstown

  • Rātā — Josh Emett; the big splurge.
  • The Bunker — dark, cosy, the perfect winter dinner.
  • Rodd & Gunn — lakefront, cosy, great cocktails.
  • SODA — Spanish shared plates by the lake.
  • Public Italian Kitchen — pasta, family-friendly.
  • Fergburger — the icon, late, 2 min from the door.
  • Ayrburn / Amisfield — the Lake Hayes lunch, e-bike day.

Wānaka

  • Bistro Gentil — lake-view fine dining; the splurge.
  • Muttonbird — the town’s standout small plates.
  • Francesca’s Italian — generous, a family favourite.
  • arc — creative set menu, great value.
  • Treehouse — boutique “trust the chef”.
  • Scroggin — top coffee & pastries (brunch).
29 June – 9 July 2026
Bring the thermals. See you on the mountain.
Family trip plan · Queenstown & Wānaka, New Zealand. Photos via Google Maps contributors.
